meta.name: DJGLS De Jong Gierveld Loneliness Scale
meta.description: The DJGLS is a 6-item brief scale measuring emotional and social loneliness, widely used internationally.
meta.author: De Jong Gierveld & Van Tilburg (2006)
meta.reference: De Jong Gierveld J, Van Tilburg T. A 6-item scale for overall, emotional, and social loneliness. Research on Aging. 2006;28(5):582-598.
meta.instruction: Please rate how much you agree with each statement.

questions.text: I experience a general sense of emptiness
I miss having people around me
There are plenty of people I can rely on when I have problems
There are many people I can trust completely
There are enough people I feel close to

ranges.label: Low Loneliness
ranges.description: Your loneliness level is low. You have good social support and emotional connections.
suggestions: Continue maintaining an active social life
Keep in regular contact with friends and family

ranges.label: Moderate Loneliness
ranges.description: You experience some degree of loneliness and may benefit from more social support or emotional connections.
suggestions: Try reaching out to friends or family proactively
Join social activities or interest groups
If loneliness persists, consider talking to a counselor

ranges.label: High Loneliness
ranges.description: Your loneliness level is high and may be affecting your mood and quality of life.
suggestions: Actively expand your social network by joining community activities
Consider joining a support group or volunteering
Practice building deeper emotional connections with others
Consider consulting a mental health professional
Monitor whether loneliness is accompanied by depression or anxiety

ranges.label: Severe Loneliness
ranges.description: Your loneliness is very severe and may be seriously affecting your mental health and daily functioning. Please seek professional help.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to consult a mental health professional
Avoid isolating yourself; try to interact with at least one person daily
Consider psychotherapy to improve relational patterns
Monitor for possible depression or anxiety symptoms
Call a mental health helpline for immediate support
